composition.Programmer 1:
Fix and complete the backend code in main.py
- Ensure the Flask app correctly locates and loads all HTML templates from the 'templates' directory.
- Verify and fix any path or configuration issues causing TemplateNotFound errors, especially for 'dashboard.html'.
- Maintain all existing backend functionalities  routing for all nine pages, reading/writing local text files (articles.txt, categories.txt, bookmarks.txt, comments.txt, trending.txt), and serving templates with appropriate data.
- Ensure the app runs on local port 5000 with the Dashboard page accessible at route '/'.
- Coordinate with Programmer 2 to confirm that template files are correctly named and placed in the 'templates' directory.
composition.Programmer 2:
Implement and organize all frontend HTML templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Develop and finalize the frontend HTML templates for all nine pages  dashboard.html, article_catalog.html, article_details.html, bookmarks.html, comments.html, write_comment.html, trending.html, category.html, search_results.html.
- Ensure all templates include the specified elements with their respective IDs as per the requirements document.
- Confirm that all templates are saved in the 'templates' directory located in the same directory as main.py, so Flask can find and render them properly.
- Use Flask route URLs (not direct HTML file names) for all links and form actions.
- Coordinate with Programmer 1 to ensure backend routes and template names match exactly.
